2604.06831.title: Towards Privacy-Preserving Large Language Model: Text-free Inference Through Alignment and Adaptation
2604.06831.paper_summary: Current LLM-based services typically require users to submit raw text regardless of its sensitivity. While intuitive, such practice introduces substantial privacy risks, as unauthorized access may expose personal, medical, or legal information. Although prior defenses strived to mitigate these risks, they often incur substantial computational overhead and degrade model performance. To overcome this privacy-efficiency trade-off, we introduce Privacy-Preserving Fine-Tuning (PPFT), a novel training pipeline that eliminates the need for transmitting raw prompt text while maintaining a favorable balance between privacy preservation and model utility for both clients and service providers. Our approach operates in two stages: first, we train a client-side encoder together with a server-side projection module and LLM, enabling the server to condition on k-pooled prompt embeddings instead of raw text; second, we fine-tune the projection module and LLM on private, domain-specific data using noise-injected embeddings, allowing effective adaptation without exposing plain text prompts and requiring access to the decoder's internal parameters. Extensive experiments on domain-specific and general benchmarks demonstrate that PPFT achieves a striking balance between privacy and utility, maintaining competitive performance with minimal degradation compared to noise-free upper bounds.
2604.06831.paper_summary_zh: 目前基於LLM的服務通常要求用戶提交原始文本，無論其敏感性如何。雖然這種做法直觀，但卻引入了相當大的隱私風險，因為未經授權的訪問可能會暴露個人、醫療或法律信息。儘管之前的防禦措施努力減輕這些風險，但它們通常會產生相當大的計算開銷並降低模型性能。為了克服這種隱私與效率的權衡，我們引入了隱私保護微調（PPFT），這是一個新穎的訓練流程，消除了傳輸原始提示文本的需要，同時為客戶和服務提供商保持隱私保護與模型效用之間的良好平衡。我們的方法分為兩個階段：首先，我們訓練一個客戶端編碼器，並與服務器端投影模塊和LLM一起運作，使服務器能夠基於k-池化的提示嵌入而不是原始文本進行條件處理；其次，我們在私有的、特定領域的數據上使用注入噪聲的嵌入微調投影模塊和LLM，從而實現有效的適應，而不暴露純文本提示並要求訪問解碼器的內部參數。在特定領域和一般基準上的廣泛實驗表明，PPFT在隱私和效用之間達到了驚人的平衡，保持了與無噪聲上限相比的競爭性能，且降級最小。
2604.06831.author: Jeongho Yoon et.al.
2604.06831.authors: Jeongho Yoon, Chanhee Park, Yongchan Chun, Hyeonseok Moon, Heuiseok Lim
